Constraints: Flit formats must match negotiated parameters; Multi-protocol NOP insertion rules must be maintained; Retry buffer size must be respected.
Coverage Method: Functional Coverage (Covergroups for CRC error types, retry trigger events, retry buffer occupancy, and multi-protocol NOP insertion during retry) and Code Coverage (Statement, Branch, FSM).
Description: The D2D Adapter must implement reliable data transfer by performing CRC computation on outgoing flits and managing a Retry mechanism. This involves storing flits in a Retry Buffer to allow for retransmission in the event of a CRC error detected on the receiving end. In multi-protocol configurations, the retry mechanism must also respect NOP insertion rules to prevent consecutive flits from the same protocol stack.
Pass/Fail Criteria: All flits sent via FDI must eventually be received correctly at the destination FDI after any necessary retries. No data corruption or loss should occur. CRC errors must trigger a retry, and NOP insertion rules must be strictly followed during retransmission in multi-protocol modes.
Randomization Constraints and Rationale: Randomize CRC error bit positions to ensure all CRC polynomial coverage; randomize flit types and protocol stack IDs to ensure retry logic works across all multiplexed streams.
Scoreboard and Checker: The scoreboard must maintain a 'shadow' Retry Buffer containing all transmitted flits. It must compare incoming FDI flits against the expected sequence, accounting for potential retries and the insertion of NOP flits in multi-protocol modes.
Sequence Implementation Notes: Implement a specialized Error Injection Sequence that can target specific bits in the RDI flit header or body to trigger various CRC error scenarios.
Sequences Coverage: Coverage of all CRC error patterns, retry buffer full/empty conditions, retry during link state transitions, and retry during multi-protocol multiplexing/throttling.
Test Case Coverage: CRC error injection, Retry buffer overflow/underflow, Retry during NOP insertion (Multi-protocol), Retry during data stream pause/restart, Retry during bandwidth throttling.
Testcase Design: Design sequences that inject bit-level errors into the RDI interface to simulate CRC failures and monitor the FDI interface for successful retransmission.
Testcase Inputs: Valid Flits (FDI), Corrupted Flits (RDI), Multi_Protocol_Enable parameter, Throttling parameters.
Testcase Outputs: Corrected data at the receiver FDI, Retry control signals on RDI, NOP flits on RDI (if multi-protocol).
Testcase Steps: 1. Configure Adapter for single or multi-protocol mode. 2. Drive valid flit sequence via FDI. 3. Inject CRC error on the RDI interface. 4. Monitor RDI for Retry request/signal. 5. Verify that the Adapter retransmits the correct flit from the Retry Buffer. 6. In multi-protocol mode, verify NOP flits are inserted correctly during the retry process.
UVM Components Involved: FDI Agent (Sequencer, Driver, Monitor), RDI Agent (Sequencer, Driver, Monitor), Error Injection Agent, Scoreboard, Predictor, Register Model (if applicable).
Verification Goal: To verify that the D2D Adapter correctly computes CRC, detects CRC errors, triggers the retry mechanism, retransmits the correct data from the Retry Buffer, and maintains protocol integrity (including NOP insertion) during retry operations.
